name: T. Tertius Noble
abstract: Thomas Tertius Noble (May 5, 1867 – May 4, 1953) was an English-born organist and composer, who lived in the United States for the latter part of his career. He served as organist and choirmaster at a number of churches including Ely Cathedral and York Minster in England and St Thomas Episcopal Church in New York City.

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: I started studying theory with the organist T. Tertius Noble, a very great musician... he asked me to turn pages for him at his weekly organ recitals... All of that cumulatively enriched me and made me move so fast as a studying young musician
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
